_meta.description: Mapping of finding types to Article 21(2) risk-management measures in the EU NIS2 Directive. References are the relevant sub-paragraphs of Article 21(2), which lists the minimum cybersecurity risk-management measures required of essential and important entities.
_meta.mapping_rationale: Article 21(2) is the operative provision for risk-management measures. Each finding maps to one or more of its sub-paragraphs (a) through (j). Where a finding legitimately implicates multiple sub-paragraphs, all are listed. This mapping is defensible but interpretive: NIS2 does not prescribe specific technical controls, and a compliance officer or auditor may reasonably arrive at a different mapping. The mapping is documented here to make the reasoning traceable.

audit_notes.S3_PUBLIC_VIA_ACL: 21(2)(i) is the primary requirement — an AllUsers grant is a direct failure of access control policies. This mapping is unambiguous.
audit_notes.S3_PUBLIC_ACCESS_BLOCK_DISABLED: Two references. 21(2)(e) covers security in ongoing system maintenance — PAB is a configuration choice that lives here. 21(2)(f) covers assessing the effectiveness of measures — PAB is one of the mechanisms by which an organisation ensures its access controls remain effective under future changes.
audit_notes.S3_ENCRYPTION_DISABLED: 21(2)(h) explicitly names cryptography and encryption. This mapping is unambiguous — NIS2 requires organisations to have policies for encryption use, and absent encryption on stored data is a direct policy gap.

S3_PUBLIC_VIA_ACL.label: Access control policies and asset management

S3_PUBLIC_ACCESS_BLOCK_DISABLED.label: Security in the acquisition, development and maintenance of network and information systems
Policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures

S3_ENCRYPTION_DISABLED.label: Policies and procedures regarding the use of cryptography and encryption
